attacking_style: Patient, reactive approach that follows dangerous late attacks rather than initiating. Wins by finishing stronger than those who opened the move, suggesting superior power reserves in final kilometers.
team_tactics: Limited team support evident - relies on individual positioning and wheel selection rather than organized team setup or tempo control. Opportunistic rather than orchestrated.
terrain_preference: Suited to situations requiring sustained power after following attacks. Benefits from selective racing where tracking key moves matters more than explosive accelerations.
vulnerability: Dependent on others initiating attacks to create winning opportunities. May struggle when required to animate racing or defend position without teammate support in grand tours.
key_patterns: Follows wheels of dangerous attackers rather than initiating moves himself
Wins by outlasting original attackers with superior finishing power
Shows tactical patience and maturity beyond typical first-time grand tour stage winner
form_trajectory: positive: maiden grand tour stage win at 26 demonstrates readiness for three-week racing and mature tactical execution
